assembly_instructions: Skateboard-style open-source electric-vehicle chassis. A rectangular welded-steel floor frame sits on four wheels at the corners, each with its own shock-absorber and brake caliper. A rack-and-pinion steering bar spans the front. Flat battery modules are bolted under the floor. At the rear, a cylindrical electric motor feeds a reduction gearbox that drives a transverse drive shaft to the rear wheels. Motor controller, BMS, and a DC/DC converter are mounted on the floor between the driver compartment and the battery.

info.japanese_name: TABBY EVO（オープンソース EV プラットフォーム）
info.english_name: Open Motors TABBY EVO — Open-Source Electric Vehicle Platform
info.summary: Italian-designed open-hardware 'skateboard' EV chassis. A modular, rolling platform intended as a starting point for EV startups, OEM concept builds, and university teaching.
info.description: TABBY EVO is the second-generation open-source electric-vehicle platform from OSVehicle / Open Motors. It is a 'skateboard' rolling chassis — a welded-steel floor frame with suspension, steering, brakes, battery, motor, and electronics all pre-integrated, ready to take any body shell bolted on top. The architecture follows a rear-drive layout with a single cylindrical electric motor feeding a reduction gearbox and a transverse drive shaft to the rear wheels, while the battery pack sits flat under the floor between the front and rear axles. A motor controller, battery management system (BMS), and a DC/DC converter ride on the floor beside the battery. Full CAD, bill of materials, wiring diagrams, and assembly manuals are released under CC BY-SA 4.0 so that others can build, fork, or commercialize the platform.
info.operator: Open Motors community + EV startups using TABBY as a reference chassis
info.contract_value: Reference BOM published; no fixed list price
info.status: Open-source; documents and CAD archived and publicly downloadable

facts.label: Powertrain
facts.value: Electric motor → reduction gearbox → transverse drive shaft (rear wheels)

facts.label: Electronics
facts.value: Motor controller + DC/DC converter on floor

facts.label: License
facts.value: CC BY-SA 4.0 (all CAD, BOM, and docs)

sources.title: Open Motors — TABBY EVO product page

parts.name: Skateboard Chassis
parts.role: Welded-steel floor frame that unifies all subsystems and becomes the mounting surface for any body shell.

parts.name: Wheel (Rear-Left)
parts.role: Rear-left wheel, driven by the drive shaft.

parts.name: Wheel (Rear-Right)
parts.role: Rear-right wheel, driven by the drive shaft.

parts.name: Steering Rack
parts.role: Rack-and-pinion steering bar between the two front wheels; steering column ties into the driver compartment above.

parts.name: Suspension Strut (representative)
parts.role: Coil-over shock absorber at the front-left corner. All four corners carry one; shown here at the FL for clarity.

parts.name: Brake Caliper + Disc (representative)
parts.role: Disc brake at the front-left wheel (representative — all four corners carry one).

parts.name: Under-Floor Battery Pack
parts.role: Flat Li-ion battery pack bolted to the underside of the chassis between the axles.

parts.name: Electric Motor
parts.role: AC electric traction motor. Drives the rear axle through the reduction gearbox.

parts.name: Reduction Gearbox
parts.role: Single-speed reduction gearbox. Converts motor torque to wheel-level torque.

parts.name: Rear Drive Shaft
parts.role: Transverse drive shaft between the gearbox and the rear wheels.

parts.name: Motor Controller
parts.role: Three-phase inverter / motor controller. Converts DC battery power into the AC drive waveform.

parts.name: Battery Management System
parts.role: BMS — monitors per-cell voltage/temperature, balances cells, isolates the pack on faults.

parts.name: DC/DC Converter
parts.role: Steps the high-voltage traction pack down to 12 V for the low-voltage accessory bus.
